title: AI Datacenter Build-Out Monitor
subtitle: Longitudinal tracker of the physical layer under the AI race — operators, sites, power deals, and the gap between announced and operational capacity.
scope: Hyperscalers, AI labs with their own infrastructure, merchant operators, and state-backed plays. Tracks campuses ≥500 MW planned, power-purchase agreements, interconnect queues, chip-allocation signals, and adoption indicators at the capacity-delivery layer — not at the strategic-posture layer, which lives in the AI Labs Watcher.
sourceCoverage: April 2026 baseline from public sources — press releases, earnings commentary, FERC and NRC filings, ISO capacity-auction reports, Data Center Frontier / Data Center Dynamics / Utility Dive coverage, and local press on specific sites.
objective: Track the delivery of AI compute infrastructure as it moves from announcement to operational capacity, so any reader at any period check-in sees which operators are vertically integrating, which campuses are actually energized versus merely announced, which power-supply arrangements are contractually firm versus aspirational, and where the announced-to-operational gap is widening or closing.
successCondition: A reader opening the doc at the end of any period knows the current lifecycle state of every tracked campus and power deal, the discrete moves each operator made this period with intent and outcome, the latest indicator values dated against public sources, and which operators moved on the position map.

objectiveFacets.name: Operator postures current
objectiveFacets.description: Each tracked operator's infrastructure posture reflects behaviour observed in the current period.

objectiveFacets.name: Site lifecycle current
objectiveFacets.description: Every tracked campus has a current lifecycle status, updated against a dated public source.

objectiveFacets.name: Power deals current
objectiveFacets.description: Every tracked PPA, nuclear restart, or on-site generation arrangement has a current contractual lifecycle status.

objectiveFacets.name: Moves logged
objectiveFacets.description: Every major public move by a tracked operator in the period is recorded with dateOf, intent, and outcome.

objectiveFacets.name: Indicators current
objectiveFacets.description: Numeric indicators — especially announced-versus-operational capacity — reflect the latest prints with as-of dates.

objectiveFacets.name: Predictions tracked
objectiveFacets.description: Every open 'what comes next' claim has a live resolution status.

objectiveFacets.name: Map current
objectiveFacets.description: Every tracked operator has a position on the map; prior-period positions are preserved so movement is visible.

objectiveFacets.name: Period attribution
objectiveFacets.description: Every update is attributable to a named monitoring period.

periods.summary: Baseline — 11 operators tracked, 15 major campuses scoped, 7 power-supply deals logged, 8 moves this period, 8 indicators dashboarded, 5 predictions open. Anchored on the ~16 GW announced / ~5 GW under construction 2026 US gap, ERCOT 233 GW large-load queue, and the Q1 nuclear-PPA cluster.

invariants.name: Operator posture cites a public source
invariants.statement: Every operator card must cite at least one public signal dated in the current or prior period.

invariants.name: Every site has an operator and a location

invariants.name: Site capacity values are dated
invariants.statement: Every site card's planned and operational capacity values must be supported by a dated public source.

invariants.name: Every power deal names its counterparty
invariants.statement: Every power-deal card must name the offtaker and the generator or utility counterparty.

invariants.name: Every move has a dateOf and byCompany
invariants.statement: Every strategic-move-log card must carry a dateOf and a byCompany reference.

invariants.name: Every indicator is dated
invariants.statement: Every indicator card must have a latestValue with an as-of date and a source.

invariants.name: Every prediction carries a resolution status
invariants.statement: Every prediction rung must carry a resolution status with rationale.

invariants.name: Map covers every tracked operator
invariants.statement: The position map must include a card for every operator in the operators section.

invariants.name: Every source has a publication date
invariants.statement: Every citation card must carry a publishedAt field.

callouts.title: What this tracker is and isn't
items: Built entirely from public sources — press releases, earnings commentary, FERC and NRC filings, ISO capacity-auction reports, Data Center Frontier / Data Center Dynamics / Utility Dive coverage, local press on specific sites.
Tracks campuses ≥500 MW planned. Sub-500 MW sites and merchant single-rack facilities are out of scope.
Announced and operational capacity are always carried as separate fields. Announcements are not deliveries.
Delays are captured as a narrative field, not collapsed into lifecycle status. A project three years late is still in construction, not cancelled.

callouts.title: The core question
items: Of ~16 GW of US datacenter projects targeting 2026 opening, public research puts only ~5 GW under actual construction. This monitor exists to watch that gap widen or close in real time at the site level.

sections.title: Tracked operators
sections.rationale: Around a dozen operators tracked by infrastructure posture — hyperscalers, AI labs running their own iron, merchant operators, and state-backed plays. This section captures strategy at the operator level; the site and power-deal sections capture delivery at the asset level.

data.name: Amazon Web Services
data.geography: US-heavy · Indiana, Pennsylvania, Virginia, Ohio core
data.strategicPosture: Integrated hyperscaler with Trainium silicon play
data.currentBet: Largest announced capex at $200B for 2026. Project Rainier Indiana operational with ~500K Trainium2 chips serving Anthropic, targeting ~1M by year-end. Amazon–Talen Susquehanna 1,920 MW nuclear PPA transitioning to front-of-meter post-Spring 2026 refueling. Competing on custom silicon (Trainium2/3) while maintaining Nvidia exposure.
activeInitiatives: Project Rainier Indiana scale from 500K to 1M Trainium2 chips
Trainium3 launch targeted for first half of 2026

notes.text: CNBC, DCD, Data Center Knowledge on Project Rainier activation; Talen Energy IR on Susquehanna PPA; Amazon 2026 capex guidance.

data.name: Microsoft Azure
data.strategicPosture: Power-constrained hyperscaler pivoting to nuclear; rationing GPU access by customer tier
data.currentBet: ~$80B unfulfilled Azure backlog reported — bottleneck is power components (transformers, switchgear, batteries) with 5-year lead times, not GPU supply or demand. First Big Tech nuclear restart signed (Three Mile Island / Crane Clean Energy Center, 835 MW, 20-year PPA, 2027–2028 restart target). Capex ~$100B+ 2026 with the delivery question louder than the budget one. On the customer side Azure is now rationing GPU access by tier: OpenAI/Anthropic at the top with bespoke clusters, Fortune-500 Azure-native tenants next, smaller firms with existing relationships behind them, and everyone else in a weeks-to-months queue with a strict use-it-or-lose-it utilisation rule.
data.evolutionSinceLastPeriod: Customer-allocation posture clarified: Azure now requires ~1,000-core minimum reservations on Nvidia Blackwell at tens-of-millions over 1–3 year terms, and explicitly publishes that revenue growth is constrained by GPU reservations for internal Copilot serving. The Information reports under-utilised clusters being reclaimed and pushed to the back of the queue.
activeInitiatives: Three Mile Island / Crane CCEC restart preparation
Mount Pleasant WI and Ohio campus expansions
OpenAI commercial relationship at new scale post-Stargate
Tiered GPU-allocation framework with use-it-or-lose-it utilisation enforcement

notes.title: The Azure backlog is the single clearest signal of the industry's power problem
items: Not a demand softness. Not a chip shortage. Transformers, switchgear, and batteries with multi-year lead times are the binding constraint.

notes.text: The Information, 'Inside Nvidia's GPU Supply Crunch' (Aaron Holmes, Anissa Gardizy, April 2026): tier structure, ~1,000-core Blackwell reservation minimums, and use-it-or-lose-it enforcement. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tq84OErx-OM

data.name: Google Cloud / Alphabet
data.geography: Global · Iowa, Nevada, Oregon, Singapore, Europe
data.strategicPosture: Vertically-integrated with TPU play and nuclear-forward posture
data.currentBet: $175–185B 2026 capex (revised upward three times from $71–73B 2025 starting estimate). Kairos Power SMR partnership targeting 500 MW fleet by early 2030s. Anthropic TPU commitment up to 1M chips disclosed October 2025. Uses Google distribution — Search, Android, Workspace, Pixel — to deliver AI without depending on standalone product wins.
activeInitiatives: TPU v5/v6 scale-out for Anthropic 1M-chip commitment

notes.text: Alphabet earnings guidance Q4 2025 and Q1 2026; Kairos Power SMR announcements; Anthropic–Google Cloud October 2025 release.

data.name: Meta
data.strategicPosture: Aggressive vertical integration via custom-built campuses + utility co-financing
data.currentBet: $115–135B 2026 capex. Richland Parish Louisiana (Hyperion) is the signature bet — 2 GW / 4M sq ft / $27B with Meta financing 5.2 GW of new Entergy gas plants, 240 miles of 500-kV transmission, and 2.5 GW of renewables. Prineville OR and Mesa AZ existing operational anchor. Meta Superintelligence Labs (Feb 2026) a parallel talent push — capability and capacity wagers running together.
activeInitiatives: Entergy 7-plant gas buildout fast-tracked by Louisiana PSC April 2026
Llama 4 training at scale on Meta-owned iron

notes.text: Meta Data Centers official; Louisiana ED; Entergy / LA PSC filings; ENR; Fox 8; Fortune coverage.

data.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
data.sector: Hyperscaler · late entrant with Stargate position
data.strategicPosture: Stargate consortium partner leveraging existing database + enterprise footprint
data.currentBet: ~$50B 2026 capex, +136% YoY. Founding Stargate partner alongside OpenAI and SoftBank, with active infrastructure role at Abilene TX. Bet is that AI-era infrastructure demand reshuffles the cloud leaderboard enough for OCI to close the gap with AWS/Azure/GCP using the Stargate-scale commitments as a lever.
activeInitiatives: Stargate Abilene co-build with OpenAI and SoftBank
OCI regional expansion funded by AI workload demand
Stargate five new US sites rollout

notes.text: Oracle earnings Q1 2026; Stargate LLC filings; Reuters / FT coverage.

data.name: OpenAI / Stargate LLC
data.sector: AI lab with dedicated infrastructure consortium
data.strategicPosture: Consortium-scale infrastructure play decoupling capacity from any single hyperscaler
data.currentBet: $500B announced Stargate commitment over four years. Abilene I at 1.2 GW targeting mid-2026 energization; Abilene II another 1 GW; five new US sites announced pushing toward 10 GW. Stargate UAE adds 1 GW (200 MW Q3 2026) as the first non-US node of the same architecture. The entire argument is that consortium capital + multi-site concurrent construction can beat the hyperscaler power-backlog problem.
activeInitiatives: Abilene I Phase 2 (6 buildings) energization mid-2026
Five new US Stargate sites buildout

notes.text: OpenAI Stargate announcements Jan 2026; Data Center Frontier; Texas Standard; Aterio; Stargate LLC Wikipedia.

data.name: xAI
data.geography: Memphis TN core · Southaven MS expansion
data.currentBet: Colossus Memphis at 2 GW / 230K GPUs (150K H100, 50K H200, 30K GB200) operational. Colossus 2 targeting 550K GB200/GB300 nodes (>1M GPUs) with first nodes coming online early 2026. Southaven MS site acquired for third campus. Stated target of 50M H100-equivalent GPUs within 5 years. Power via on-site gas turbines at Memphis — the speed-over-clean archetype.

notes.text: Introl; Tom's Hardware; FinancialContent / WRAL; Tweaktown on Musk public statements.

data.name: CoreWeave
data.strategicPosture: Merchant-scale neocloud betting on hyperscaler-tenancy durability — explicitly off the 2023-era startup-haven pitch
data.currentBet: $30–35B 2026 capex. 43 datacenters / 850 MW active / ~600K GPUs (mostly H100/H200, rising B200/B300 share) year-end 2025. $14B Meta deal and $55B total backlog. Thesis: hyperscalers will continue renting frontier capacity rather than owning all of it, especially when capex-per-GW at merchant scale undercuts internal build. Structurally exposed if hyperscalers vertically integrate further. The 2023 positioning as the GPU-access alternative for startups frozen out of AWS/Azure/GCP has been quietly dropped — capacity now flows to the largest customers, leaving an emerging gap that inference-provider startups are starting to target.
data.evolutionSinceLastPeriod: Posture clarified: the 2023-era startup-haven pitch is no longer the marketing position. The Information confirms (April 2026) that CoreWeave and peers now prioritise the largest customers, mirroring the hyperscaler tier dynamic rather than countering it.

notes.text: CoreWeave capacity plan blog; Next Platform financial engineering analysis; Stansberry Research on $55B backlog.
The Information, 'Inside Nvidia's GPU Supply Crunch' (April 2026) on the neo-cloud strategic pivot since the 2023 baseline. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tq84OErx-OM

data.name: Nebius
data.strategicPosture: Ramp-first merchant with contracted long-lead power
data.currentBet: 170 MW connected year-end 2025, targeting 800 MW–1 GW connected by end 2026. Contracted 3+ GW of power and announced nine new datacenters. Independence MO 400-acre AI factory can support 1.2 GW. Bet is speed to energized capacity via pre-contracted power and Kansas City grid access.
activeInitiatives: 800 MW–1 GW connected capacity target end-2026

notes.text: Nebius investor materials; Techi.com; Yahoo Finance Nebius vs CoreWeave coverage.

data.name: Lambda Labs
data.currentBet: Kansas City MO AI factory launching 2026 with 10K NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs, 24 MW initial capacity expandable to 100 MW. Developer-first GPU cloud pitch; smaller than CoreWeave and Nebius but with tighter developer-onboarding loops. Structurally the most exposed to merchant-hyperscaler squeeze.
activeInitiatives: Kansas City AI factory launch with 10K B300 Ultra GPUs
Expansion path from 24 MW to 100 MW

notes.text: Lambda announcements; MLQ.ai neocloud infrastructure research.

data.name: G42 / Stargate UAE
data.strategicPosture: Sovereign infrastructure play with US consortium co-build
data.currentBet: Stargate UAE 1 GW compute cluster operated with OpenAI, Oracle, Cisco, SoftBank, NVIDIA. First 200 MW phase targeting Q3 2026 completion. Broader 10 sq mile UAE–US AI infrastructure campus reaches 5 GW planned. Powered by nuclear, solar, and natural gas mix — the sovereign archetype with all three energy options in play.
activeInitiatives: Stargate UAE Phase 1 200 MW Q3 2026 delivery

notes.text: G42 press; Silicon UK; Gulf News; The National; DCD on UAE–US 5 GW plan.

sections.title: Major campuses
sections.rationale: One card per major campus (≥500 MW planned) moving through the lifecycle. The gap between plannedCapacity and operationalCapacity at card level is the primary signal this section carries.

delayVsPlan.text: On schedule as of April 2026 per Stargate public updates. Powered via SB Energy accelerated procurement plus a new substation in Milam TX.

milestones.text: Jan 2026 Stargate consortium formalised; construction proceeding on buildings 3–8 of Phase I.

notes.text: Data Center Frontier; IntuitionLabs; DCD Stargate coverage.

delayVsPlan.text: No announced slippage. Phase II began construction March 2025.

milestones.text: Six additional buildings under active construction. Substation and transmission work concurrent.

notes.text: Data Center Frontier; Texas Standard.

data.name: Stargate — five new US sites
data.location: Multiple US states (disclosed in January 2026 release)
data.plannedCapacity: ~5 GW aggregate pushing Stargate toward 10 GW total

delayVsPlan.text: No construction yet — site selection and permitting phase. Gap risk high given the base rate on this vintage.

milestones.text: Public announcement bundle tied to Stargate consortium scaling from 2 sites to 7.

notes.text: OpenAI five new sites blog; DCF / Aterio.

delayVsPlan.text: On/ahead of schedule — the outlier in the cohort. Powered by on-site gas turbines, which is the speed-over-clean archetype.

milestones.text: Jan 2026: xAI publicly confirmed 2 GW total capacity and 555K purchase cumulative across Colossus 1 + 2 pipeline at ~$18B.

notes.text: Introl xAI Colossus coverage; Tom's Hardware.

milestones.text: Jan 2026 confirmation of 550K-node target; first nodes energizing.

data.plannedCapacity: Undisclosed — projected as third campus toward 1M-GPU total by late 2026

data.operationalCapacity: Partial — delivery constrained by power components

milestones.text: Ongoing construction; capacity not disclosed publicly.

sections.title: Power-supply deals
sections.rationale: One card per PPA, nuclear restart, SMR agreement, or on-site generation arrangement. Lifecycle is contractual and regulatory rather than physical.

delayVsPlan.text: First US retired reactor brought back to serve a single corporate client. NRC licensing is the critical-path risk; project officially renamed Crane Clean Energy Center.

milestones.text: 20-year fixed-price PPA signed; NRC restart application progressing; restoration capex funded.

notes.title: The reference deal for hyperscaler nuclear
items: Microsoft's Crane agreement ignited the sector — Amazon–Talen and Google–Kairos both post-date it structurally.

data.name: Amazon / Talen Susquehanna
data.expectedOperational: Front-of-meter transition after Spring 2026 refueling outage

delayVsPlan.text: Restructured into front-of-meter PPA to avoid the FERC rejection that blocked the prior co-location model in Nov 2024. FERC approval not required for the new structure.

milestones.text: June 11 2025 expanded PPA announcement; transmission reconfig scheduled for Spring 2026.

notes.text: Talen IR filings; RTO Insider; Power Magazine; World Nuclear News.

delayVsPlan.text: Longest timeline of the three hyperscaler nuclear plays. SMR technology risk + NRC licensing novelty.

milestones.text: Initial design work underway; first deployment site not publicly disclosed.

notes.text: Google SMR partnership announcements; Data Center News explainer; PowerMag SMR gamble.

sections.title: Strategic moves this period
sections.rationale: Dated events by tracked operators — site announcements, PPA signings, NRC approvals, ISO interconnect rulings, land purchases, permit outcomes, chip-allocation disclosures.

data.name: Stargate consortium formalised
data.intent: Lock in multi-hundred-billion-dollar compute capacity through a consortium JV, decoupling OpenAI's capacity ceiling from Microsoft Azure alone.
data.observedEffect: Repositions OpenAI from Azure-dependent to consortium-scale. Consortium immediately expanded to 7 sites (Abilene I/II + 5 new).

notes.text: Reuters / FT coverage of Stargate announcement.

data.name: Stargate adds five new US sites
data.intent: Scale Stargate from 2 to 7 US sites pushing toward 10 GW aggregate capacity.
data.observedEffect: Site selection announced; construction not yet started on the new five. High gap risk given base rate.

notes.text: OpenAI five new sites release; Data Center Frontier.

data.name: xAI confirms Colossus at 2 GW / 555K GPUs
data.intent: Public confirmation of fastest-to-operational supercluster at single-site scale.
data.observedEffect: Colossus 1 operational at 2 GW; Colossus 2 energizing with 550K-node target.

notes.text: Introl Jan 2026 piece; Tom's Hardware.

data.name: AWS activates Project Rainier cluster
data.intent: Bring ~500K Trainium2 chips online for Anthropic at $11B Indiana campus.
data.observedEffect: Indiana operational; path to 1M chips by year-end 2026 publicly committed.

notes.text: CNBC; DCD; AWS blog; Data Center Knowledge.

data.name: Louisiana PSC fast-tracks Entergy / Meta 7-plant buildout
data.intent: Clear regulatory path for 5.2 GW of new gas generation tied to Meta Richland Parish.
data.observedEffect: Fast-track approval lowers delivery risk on the largest tracked site. Sets precedent for utility-scale co-financing of hyperscaler AI builds.

notes.text: Fox 8 Live; NOLA.com.

data.name: Amazon expands Talen Susquehanna PPA to 1,920 MW
data.intent: Secure 1,920 MW of carbon-free nuclear capacity through 2042 after FERC rejected the prior co-location structure.
data.observedEffect: Front-of-meter restructure avoids FERC approval hurdle. Transmission reconfig on track for Spring 2026.

notes.text: Talen IR; RTO Insider; Power Magazine.

data.name: DOE directs FERC to accelerate datacenter interconnection
data.intent: Relieve the large-load interconnection backlog that is constraining datacenter delivery.
data.observedEffect: Implementation through PJM Expedited Interconnection Track by August 2026; other ISOs pending.

notes.text: White & Case insight alert; Utility Dive.

data.name: PJM Expedited Interconnection Track for large loads
data.intent: Shorten timeline for large-load interconnection requests — the majority of which are datacenters.
data.observedEffect: Rule change in flight; first benefit expected to land in H2 2026.

notes.text: Zero Emission Grid blog; Utility Dive.

data.name: Azure formalises tiered GPU allocation with use-it-or-lose-it enforcement
data.intent: Ration scarce Blackwell capacity in favour of OpenAI/Anthropic and Fortune-500 Azure-native tenants while Microsoft also reserves a growing share for internal Copilot serving.
data.observedEffect: Smaller customers face weeks-to-months waits for new clusters and minimum reservations of ~1,000 cores at tens of millions of dollars over 1–3 years; clusters idle for hours overnight are reclaimed and pushed to the back of the queue. Azure publicly pins constrained revenue growth on this rationing.

notes.text: The Information, 'Inside Nvidia's GPU Supply Crunch' (Aaron Holmes, Anissa Gardizy, April 2026). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tq84OErx-OM

data.name: Neo-clouds pivot from startup-haven positioning to serving the largest customers
data.intent: Capture revenue scale by chasing OpenAI/Anthropic-class customers, the inverse of the 2023-era pitch as the GPU-access alternative for startups frozen out of hyperscalers.
data.observedEffect: Startup access to neo-cloud capacity has materially eroded over three years; The Information reports inference-provider startups starting to position for the abandoned mid-market. VC firms (General Catalyst, Founders Fund, Khosla portfolios) now negotiating GPU access as fund-level intermediaries.

notes.text: The Information, 'Inside Nvidia's GPU Supply Crunch' (Aaron Holmes, Anissa Gardizy, April 2026). Anissa Gardizy + Aaron Holmes co-reported the 2023 baseline. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tq84OErx-OM

sections.title: Indicator dashboard
sections.rationale: The numeric dashboard. The core indicator is the announced-versus-operational capacity gap.

data.name: US AI datacenter capacity: announced 2026 vs under construction
data.latestValue: ~16 GW announced / ~5 GW under construction (~70% announced, not-building)
data.forecast: Gap likely widens through 2026 as new announcements outpace transformer + switchgear + interconnect delivery.

data.forecast: Queue likely still rising through 2026 before DOE/FERC acceleration bites.

data.forecast: Elevated through 2027–2028 without material capacity additions.

data.name: Top-5 hyperscaler aggregate 2026 capex

notes.text: Futurum Group AI Capex 2026 analysis; company earnings guidance.

data.name: Nuclear PPAs under contract for hyperscalers
data.forecast: Rising — additional restart and SMR deals plausible through 2026–2027.

notes.text: Data Center Frontier nuclear coverage; Talen IR; Bird & Bird nuclear PPA piece.

data.name: Microsoft Azure unfulfilled datacenter backlog
data.forecast: Contracts on the books; delivery tracks power-component supply recovery.

notes.text: Microsoft filings / Q2 FY26 guidance; Tech Insider analysis.

sections.title: What comes next
sections.rationale: Open predictions about the physical layer through 2026–2028.

data.name: The announced-to-operational capacity gap widens through 2026

notes.text: Baseline: ~16 GW US announced for 2026, ~5 GW under construction.
Resolution: if Q4 2026 monitoring shows the ratio deteriorating, confirmed. If the ratio improves (more sites under construction than net new announcements), refuted.

data.name: First grid-scale nuclear restart energizes for AI load before end-2028

notes.text: Microsoft/Crane target 2027–2028; Amazon/Talen Susquehanna front-of-meter transition Spring 2026 (existing reactor).
Resolution: confirmed on first NRC-approved restart delivering AI-dedicated load. Refuted if all three 2027-target restarts slip past 2028.

data.name: At least one US state passes a hyperscale-DC permitting moratorium by end-2027

notes.text: Community friction visible in Louisiana Fortune coverage and in Memphis press around xAI gas turbines.
Resolution: confirmed if any US state passes a formal moratorium (legislative or gubernatorial executive order) on new hyperscale DC permits citing grid or environmental concerns by 2027-12-31.

data.name: A hyperscaler publicly cuts 2026 capex guidance citing siting reality

notes.text: Microsoft Azure backlog already implies delivery friction without a formal guidance cut.
Resolution: confirmed if any tracked hyperscaler revises 2026 capex down in earnings or press citing power, siting, or transformer supply. Refuted if all five trend flat or up through FY26.

data.name: A 10 GW+ state-orchestrated AI campus operational outside the US by end-2027

notes.text: Stargate UAE 5 GW campus and Saudi Arabia chip-deal pipeline; Chinese state-directed AI infra also scaling.
Resolution: confirmed on first operational deployment ≥10 GW at a single campus or coordinated campus outside the US. Refuted if end-2027 the largest non-US AI campus is still under 5 GW operational.

sections.title: Position map — power-sourcing × vertical-integration
sections.rationale: Two-axis placement. X: power-sourcing strategy (dirty/fast ← → clean/slow). Y: vertical-integration (merchant tenant ← → fully owned stack).

sections.title: Period notes
sections.rationale: One card per monitoring period summarising what shifted.

notes.text: First monitoring period. 11 operators tracked, 14 major campuses scoped across the lifecycle (with one 'fleet' card for CoreWeave below the single-site threshold), 7 power-supply deals logged, 10 moves this period, 6 indicators dashboarded, 5 predictions open. The consolidated baseline is the ~16 GW US announced / ~5 GW under construction 2026 gap, the 233 GW ERCOT large-load queue, and the Q1 2026 nuclear-PPA cluster.

Refreshed 2026-04-27 from The Information's 'Inside Nvidia's GPU Supply Crunch' — added Azure tiered-allocation posture move, neo-cloud strategic-shift move, and updated CoreWeave + Azure operator cards on the customer-allocation side of the build-out.

notes.title: Focus for 2026-H2
items: Does Stargate Abilene Phase I hit the mid-2026 full-GW energization target?
Do any of the Stargate five new US sites move from announced into permitting or construction by end-Q3?
Does the announced-to-operational ratio improve, hold, or deteriorate in the Q4 re-scan?
Does PJM's August 2026 Expedited Interconnection Track produce measurable queue throughput?
First confirmed nuclear restart date slippage — or confirmation — on the Microsoft/Crane timeline.
Does Azure's tiered GPU-allocation policy ease as Stargate / neo-cloud capacity comes online — or harden into a structural feature?
